title: Choosing Your Path: University or Vocational School?
passage:
Choosing Your Path: University or Vocational School?

Paragraph A
After high school, young people must make an important choice. Some people go to university. This is called higher education. Other people go to a vocational school. This is called vocational training. Both paths help students get jobs, but they are very different. It is important to understand these two choices before making a decision.

Paragraph B
University is a very popular choice for many students. At university, students study for three to four years. They learn from textbooks and listen to lectures in large classrooms. They study academic subjects like history, science, literature, or business. At the end of their studies, they get a degree. This degree helps them find jobs in big offices, schools, or hospitals. However, university is often very expensive, and students have to study for many years.

Paragraph C
Vocational training is different because it is very practical. At a vocational school, students do not spend all day reading books. Instead, they practice doing a specific job with their hands. For example, they learn how to build houses, fix cars, style hair, or cook food. This training is usually short, taking only one or two years. It is also cheaper than university. Students can finish their classes and start earning money quickly.

Paragraph D
Which path is better? It depends on what the student likes. If a student likes reading, writing, and doing research, university is a great choice. But if a student likes physical work and wants to start a job fast, vocational training is a great choice. Both paths can lead to a happy and successful life.

questions.instruction: Do the following statements agree with the information given in the reading passage? Write TRUE, FALSE, or NOT GIVEN.
questions.text: Students at university study for three to four years.
questions.explanation: Paragraph B states: 'At university, students study for three to four years.' This is exactly the same information.

questions.instruction: Do the following statements agree with the information given in the reading passage? Write TRUE, FALSE, or NOT GIVEN.
questions.text: University is always free for all students.
questions.explanation: Paragraph B states: 'However, university is often very expensive.' Therefore, it is not free.

questions.instruction: Do the following statements agree with the information given in the reading passage? Write TRUE, FALSE, or NOT GIVEN.
questions.text: Vocational training takes five years to finish.
questions.explanation: Paragraph C states that vocational training is 'usually short, taking only one or two years.' Therefore, five years is incorrect.

questions.instruction: Do the following statements agree with the information given in the reading passage? Write TRUE, FALSE, or NOT GIVEN.
questions.text: Students in vocational school learn how to paint pictures.
questions.explanation: Paragraph C mentions learning how to 'build houses, fix cars, style hair, or cook food', but it does not mention painting pictures.

questions.instruction: Complete the sentences below. Choose NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS from the passage for each answer.
questions.text: At university, students learn from ______ and listen to lectures.
questions.explanation: Paragraph B says: 'They learn from textbooks and listen to lectures...'

questions.instruction: Complete the sentences below. Choose NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS from the passage for each answer.
questions.text: A university degree helps people find jobs in offices, schools, or ______.
questions.explanation: Paragraph B says: 'This degree helps them find jobs in big offices, schools, or hospitals.'

questions.instruction: Complete the sentences below. Choose NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS from the passage for each answer.
questions.text: Vocational training is also called ______ school.
questions.explanation: Paragraph C says: 'Vocational training is different... It is also called trade school.'

questions.instruction: Complete the sentences below. Choose NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS from the passage for each answer.
questions.explanation: Paragraph C says: 'It is also cheaper than university.'

questions.instruction: Choose the correct letter, A, B, C or D.
questions.text: What do students do at a vocational school?

options.A: Listen to long lectures all day

questions.explanation: Paragraph C states: 'Instead, they practice doing a specific job with their hands.'

questions.instruction: Choose the correct letter, A, B, C or D.
questions.text: How long does vocational training usually take?
questions.explanation: Paragraph C states: 'This training is usually short, taking only one or two years.'

questions.instruction: Choose the correct letter, A, B, C or D.
questions.text: Who is vocational training best for?

options.B: Students who want to work in hospitals
options.D: Students who want to study history

questions.explanation: Paragraph D states: 'But if a student likes physical work and wants to start a job fast, vocational training is a great choice.'

vocabulary.meaning: education at a college or university level
skills and training for a specific job or trade
a qualification given by a university after finishing studies
relating to experience and hands-on activities rather than theory

tactics:
Hướng dẫn làm bài (Tactics)

1. Kỹ thuật tìm từ khóa trực tiếp (Scanning): Với trình độ A1-A2, các câu hỏi sử dụng từ ngữ giống hệt trong bài đọc. Hãy gạch chân từ khóa trong câu hỏi (ví dụ: 'three to four years', 'trade school') rồi tìm chính xác cụm từ đó trong văn bản.

2. Cách làm dạng True/False/Not Given: Đối với câu hỏi 'Not Given', nếu bạn không thể tìm thấy thông tin nào nói về từ khóa đó (ví dụ: 'paint pictures'), hãy chọn ngay NOT GIVEN. Không tự suy luận bên ngoài bài đọc.
